findings.description: Table 1 (rake toss sampling point coordinates) is duplicated in the HTML — it appears first as an embedded image of the original table and then again as an HTML-rendered table, creating redundant content that disrupts reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF page 6 shows Table 1 once as a formatted table. In the HTML conversion (visible around HTML chunk 5), the same table appears first as a screenshot/image and then immediately again as a properly rendered HTML table, both with the heading 'Table 1. Longitude and Latitude values for rake toss sampling points.'

findings.description: Table 2 (Secchi Disk locations and results) is duplicated in the HTML — it appears first as an embedded image of the original table and then again as an HTML-rendered table, creating redundant content that disrupts reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF page 11 shows Table 2 once as a formatted table. In the HTML conversion (visible in the last HTML chunk), the same table appears first as a screenshot/image and then immediately again as a properly rendered HTML table, both with the heading 'Table 2. Secchi Disk locations and results.'

findings.description: Table 1 content appears both as an embedded image (screenshot of the table) and as a separate HTML . The same duplication occurs for Table 2. In each case the same data is rendered twice — once as an and once as structured HTML.

findings.description: Two images (apparent screenshots of Tables 1 and 2) have empty alt text (alt=""), which marks them as decorative. If they are substantive images of tables, this is incorrect; if they are truly redundant with the HTML tables, they should be removed rather than marked decorative.
findings.evidence: HTML: appears twice — once before Table 1 and once before Table 2.
